## --- Day 15: Chiton ---

The cave roof is getting lower and you can only move in two dimensions.

*What is the lowest total risk of any path from the top left to the bottom right?*




::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(tidyverse)
library(igraph)
```

:::

```{.r .cell-code}
x = as.matrix(x)
```
:::




Create a matrix and use some kind of graph movement.




::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
define_edges = function(i, j) {
        if (i == 1 & j == 1) {
            e = list(c(1,0), c(0,1))
        } else if (i == 1 & j == 100) {
            e = list(c(1,0), c(0,-1)) 
        } else if (i == 100 & j == 1) {
            e = list(c(-1,0), c(0,1))
        } else if (i == 100 & j == 100) {
            e = list(c(-1,0), c(0,-1))
        } else if (i == 1) {
            e = list(c(1,0), c(0,1), c(0,-1))
        } else if (j == 1) {
            e = list(c(-1,0), c(1,0), c(0,1))
        } else if (i == 100) { 
            e = list(c(-1,0), c(0,1), c(0,-1))
        } else if (j == 100) { 
            e = list(c(-1,0), c(1,0), c(0,-1))
        } else {
            e = list(c(1,0), c(0,1), c(-1,0), c(0,-1))
        }
        
    return(e)
}
```
:::

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
g = make_empty_graph(n=10000, directed=TRUE)

for (i in 1:100) { 
    for (j in 1:100) { 
        # define edges that we can move on
        e = define_edges(i,j)
        for (d in e) {
            a = i + d[1]
            b = j + d[2]
            outo = (a-1)*100 + b # from this node
            into = (i-1)*100 + j # into this node
            # cat(outo, into, "\n")
            # print(into)
            g = add_edges(g, c(outo,into), weight=x[i,j])
        }
    }
}
```
:::




Solve?




::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
distances(g, 1, 10000)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}


```{.r .cell-code}
shortest_paths(g, 1, 10000)
```
